segments.title: Angled flight deck and well deck question
segments.summary: A Q&A window explains why combining an angled flight deck with a well deck is theoretically possible but operationally awkward.
segments.body: The answer separates design-study possibility from practical ship use: a well deck requires slow, docked-down operations that interfere with normal carrier flight-deck activity, making helicopters or V/STOL aircraft a better fit for amphibious aviation roles.

evidence.note: The transcript asks about combining an angled flight deck with a well deck and answers that the combination is possible in theory but poor in practical operation.

segments.question: Is it possible to have both an angled flight deck and a well deck, effectively turning an assault ship into a fleet carrier?
segments.answerShort: Possible in theory, but the slow dock-down well-deck operating mode conflicts with normal carrier flight operations, so it is usually impractical.
